purpose: Define precise client document requests for building traceable investor-advisor knowledge systems without asking for all related documents. The required unit is source provenance, not necessarily a stable document-level download URL.

forbiddenRequestPattern.reason: This creates unbounded scope, duplicates, rights problems, privacy risk, and unverifiable knowledge ingestion.

requestPrinciples.name: use-case first
requestPrinciples.rule: Every requested document must map to a named advisor use case, claim class, evaluation scenario, or UI/runtime feature.

requestPrinciples.name: source type before file names
requestPrinciples.rule: Ask for document categories and metadata first; ask for files only when the source type is required by the knowledge plan.

requestPrinciples.name: public source preferred
requestPrinciples.rule: Prefer stable public document URLs, DART receipt numbers, KRX identifiers, and official IR source pages before requesting uploaded files. When the issuer uses dynamic or session-based downloads, record the official source page, exact document title, period, local file name, checksum, and access date.

requestPrinciples.name: latest and bounded by default
requestPrinciples.rule: Default time scope is latest fiscal year, latest four quarters, latest value-up/shareholder-return materials, and latest governance baselines unless a longitudinal claim requires more.

requestPrinciples.name: rights and confidentiality explicit
requestPrinciples.rule: Every source must indicate whether it is public, client-confidential, licensed third-party, or restricted.

requestPrinciples.name: no raw dumps
requestPrinciples.rule: Do not accept unsorted email archives, chat exports, shared-drive dumps, contract folders, or press clipping folders unless a narrow claim-driven request has been approved.

requestPrinciples.name: minimum metadata required
requestPrinciples.rule: Each source must include title, issuer/publisher, date or period, source-page URL, public document URL or filing ID when available, file origin when uploaded, owner/contact when non-public, rights level, and intended use.

requestPackages.name: company identity and market identifiers
askFor: listed entity names in Korean and English
DART corp codes or DART profile links
doNotAskFor: internal org charts not used in public investor communication

requestPackages.name: financial disclosure baseline
askFor: latest four quarterly or semiannual reports
restated or corrected reports affecting the selected period
DART receipt numbers or public filing URLs
requestPackages.defaultPeriod: latest fiscal year plus latest four quarters
doNotAskFor: full historical archive unless trend analysis requires it
spreadsheet exports without source period or owner

requestPackages.name: earnings and investor presentation baseline
askFor: latest investor day or corporate presentation
official transcript or replay link if the transcript is already public
requestPackages.defaultPeriod: latest four quarters and current strategic event
doNotAskFor: audio files without transcripts unless voice-demo evaluation requires them
old IR decks unless a specific claim needs them

requestPackages.name: shareholder value and capital action baseline
askFor: spin-off, merger, split-off, or restructuring presentation
doNotAskFor: board deliberation materials unless explicitly approved for confidential analysis
draft plans not approved for model ingestion

requestPackages.name: governance and disclosure baseline
askFor: shareholder meeting materials relevant to current claims
doNotAskFor: personal data on directors beyond public disclosures
meeting minutes unless a narrow confidential workflow is approved

requestPackages.name: market-view comparison
askFor: public analyst report links or bibliographic metadata
licensed report titles and dates if the client has usage rights
internal summary note that is cleared for use
doNotAskFor: licensed report PDFs unless redistribution and model-use rights are explicit

requestPackages.name: commercial operating context
doNotAskFor: contracts, emails, or chat logs unless narrowed and approved

acceptanceCriteria: Each uploaded or linked source maps to at least one request package.
Each source has a date or covered period.
Each source has rights and confidentiality labels.
Each public source has source provenance: a source-page URL, public document URL, DART/public filing ID, or a documented reason why only local-file evidence is available.
No source is promoted to wiki/runtime context before claim-level traceability is recorded.
